text:
The glass wall before me both invites and separates. I notice others inside discussing, moving, absorbed in their own worlds, as I hover on the threshold. There’s a sense of transition—am I outside looking in, or on my way to enter? The boundaries here are physical and invisible.

Then, in the other room dominated by ideas—bright colors, experimental displays, the R+D sign—I read words that mirror my own feelings: exposure, confusion, longing for understanding. I feel reflective and slightly vulnerable, pondering how we’re all constantly navigating our own perceptions and the interpretations of others. This moment feels both isolating and connective, as if I am part of something being explored, yet uncertain how much of myself is truly seen.
